semantics: WebAssembly.compileStreaming and WebAssembly.instantiateStreaming are non-enumerable namespace methods with length one and return intrinsic Promises.
The source is assimilated as a Promise or thenable before its fulfillment is validated as an engine Response, preserving abrupt and rejected reason identity.
Only successful 200 through 299 responses are accepted; a non-Response, failed status, locked body, or already-used non-null body rejects with TypeError before compilation.
Content-Type is compared byte-case-insensitively with application/wasm after HTTP-whitespace trimming, and every parameter including an empty trailing semicolon is rejected.
The Response body is consumed through the same pull-driven Fetch Body machinery as the public body methods, so bodyUsed, locking, cancellation, and abrupt stream errors stay coherent.
Each normalized body chunk is copied directly into the issue 409 compiler lifecycle; EOF finalizes one stable engine-owned byte snapshot before queued compilation.
Streaming compilation uses the exact ordinary WebAssembly parser, validator, enabled feature profile, module owner, and CompileError conversion without a permissive alternate path.
Streaming instantiation uses the exact ordinary import resolver, linker, start-function execution, result shape, and TypeError, LinkError, RuntimeError, or original exception behavior.
compileStreaming fulfills with a WebAssembly.Module while instantiateStreaming fulfills with an ordinary object containing the compiled module and instance.
The import object is retained as a managed Value edge until the queued instantiate job completes and remains visible across collection and relocation.
Responses created in a same-VM sibling realm are accepted while the returned Promise and compiled wrapper use the calling realm's intrinsics.
Asynchronous ordering is preserved across source assimilation, body pulls, body completion, compilation, linking, and outer Promise settlement.
Every declared WebAssembly conformance profile reaches the same streaming entry point and exact decoder rather than profile-specific streaming shims.
The frozen WG1 submodule remains the reproducible MVP corpus baseline; the WG3 Web API source separately pins the streaming algorithm and MIME rule.

Focused Debug, ReleaseSafe, and TSan evidence covers split asynchronous chunks, imports, sibling realms, MIME and status rejection, locking, disturbance, malformed modules, abrupt pulls, and rejection identity.
